rationale.R1.1: The gap statement (deployment-time viewpoint shift) and contribution claim ('first method') are present but imprecisely scoped; improving the draft calls for sharpening how the thesis is articulated.
rationale.R1.2: Load-bearing: the draft opens by framing CrossViewSeg as a viewpoint-robustness solution but the conclusion retreats to calling the contribution 'a simple augmentation recipe.' These premises and conclusion are in direct tension and invalidate the logical arc.
rationale.R1.3: Load-bearing: the core claim ('first method… under arbitrary camera viewpoint shifts') is not validated by the experiments — Cityscapes-val and BDD100K-val are standard same-distribution benchmarks with no cross-viewpoint test protocol. Evidence does not support the primary claim.
rationale.R1.5: The narrative arc breaks mid-paper: sections 1–2 promise a viewpoint-generalization system; the results section discusses boundary sharpness on thin objects; the conclusion reframes the work as a training recipe. Structural reordering or reframing is required.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is a natural component of 'improve this draft'; several sentences are serviceable but below the clarity expected of a venue submission.

parsed.clarification_question: Should the improvement address substantive content issues — specifically, the mismatch between the viewpoint-robustness framing and the absence of any cross-viewpoint evaluation — or is the request limited to prose and writing quality only?
